verdict: 2026-07-01 19:41:58 PDT — Sound shape. Reformatting the four drifted shell files with `shfmt -i 2 -ci` and enforcing `shfmt -d -i 2 -ci` across all tracked `*.sh` in CI fits this repo’s conventions: the local gate remains the deliberately dependency-light bash test command in `.claude/workflow.json`, while tool-heavy checks live in CI next to ShellCheck and gitleaks. A pinned, checksum-verified shfmt binary matches the existing gitleaks trust model without adding a third-party action. Running shfmt on PRs and pushes is correct because formatting is cheap and meaningful on every `gate` event; gitleaks is PR-scoped for commit-range scanning, not as a general rule for all external tools. The `git ls-files '*.sh'` invocation is not NUL-safe, but it matches the existing ShellCheck convention and current tracked paths, so a more complex pipeline has no concrete payoff here. The whitespace-only claim should remain backed by the explicit `git diff -w main...HEAD -- '*.sh'` acceptance check rather than assumed from shfmt, and the sketch already has that guard. No design findings.
